Hero.trustSignal: Evidence-based · Clinician-reviewed · Not a prescribing tool
Hero.subtitle: Understanding your hormone health is hard. We’re making it easier. TRT Insights turns scattered lab reports into structured clinical insight — the next frontier of precision medicine.

Overview.label: Overview
Overview.patientStatement: TRT Insights is dramatically changing how people understand their own hormone therapy — every lab, every trend, every symptom, finally on <highlight>one clinical timeline</highlight>.
Overview.patientNote: Upload your history once. We normalize every biomarker against its lab-specific reference range, so you walk into every appointment prepared.
Overview.clinicianStatement: TRT Insights is dramatically shortening the path from raw lab data to clinical conversation — normalized values, trend math, and <highlight>physician-ready summaries</highlight> in seconds.
Overview.clinicianNote: A deterministic rules engine — not a black box — produces every baseline classification, so what you review is reproducible and auditable.

Technology.demoCaption: Illustrative demo data — not medical advice.
Technology.title: The Endocrine Picture: Implications for Health
Technology.body: Hormones influence nearly every dimension of health — energy, body composition, mood, cognition, longevity. Yet the data lives scattered across PDFs, portals, and spreadsheets. TRT Insights pairs AI-powered extraction with a deterministic rules engine to normalize every biomarker, track it against its lab-specific reference range, and surface the trends that matter. The implications are global: better-prepared patients, better-informed clinicians, and a higher standard for precision hormone care.
capabilities: AI extraction from lab PDFs — uncertain values flagged for review
Per-lab reference ranges and unit normalization, never global ranges
Deterministic clinical rules engine — reproducible, auditable output

Research.title: The next frontier of precision medicine

Footer.copyright: © {year} TRT Insights · Clinical decision support, not a medical device.

Layout.quote: A structured, longitudinal view of your labs — so the time with your doctor goes to decisions, not deciphering PDFs.

Forgot.title: Reset your password
Forgot.body: Password reset emails aren't enabled yet in this build. Contact your administrator to reset, or create a new account.

Safety.disclaimer: This software provides educational and organizational support only. It does not diagnose medical conditions or prescribe treatment. All treatment decisions must be made by a qualified healthcare professional.

Overview.subtitle: An overview of your recorded health data.
Overview.recentValuesDesc: The latest extracted results. Reference ranges are per-lab and shown for context only.
Overview.noResults: No lab results yet. Upload a lab report to get started.

Labs.title: Labs
Labs.subtitle: Upload lab reports. Extraction is automatic but always surfaces for your review.
Labs.uploadDesc: PDF, JPG, PNG, or HEIC. Drag & drop or browse.
Labs.reportCount: {count, plural, one {# report on file} other {# reports on file}}

LabsResults.title: Lab results
LabsResults.subtitle: All recorded values, classified against their per-lab reference ranges.
LabsResults.noResults: No results yet. Upload a lab or add values manually.

Timeline.title: Timeline
Timeline.subtitle: Every recorded event, in chronological order (GOLD §5.8).
Timeline.noEvents: No events yet. Upload a lab or add a medication to populate your timeline.

Analysis.title: Comprehensive Analysis
Analysis.summary: {analyzed, plural, one {# biomarker analyzed} other {# biomarkers analyzed}} · {outOfRange, plural, one {# out of range} other {# out of range}}
Analysis.noResults: No lab results yet. Upload or enter your results to see the analysis.
Analysis.clinicalContext: Clinical context from the knowledge base
Analysis.clinicalContextDesc: Medical entity relationships relevant to your results, from the clinical literature.
Analysis.suggestedTestsDesc: For discussion when data are incomplete.

Reports.title: Reports
Reports.subtitle: Structured clinical summaries for your healthcare provider.
Reports.generateDesc: Produces a guideline-informed summary from your recorded labs. Educational only — it does not diagnose or prescribe (GOLD §5.13).
Reports.uploadFirst: Upload and extract a lab first.
Reports.generateButton: Generate report ({count, plural, one {# value} other {# values}})
Reports.noReportsWhat: Generated reports appear here. They summarize your trends and list red flags and questions to discuss with your clinician.
Reports.noReportsNext: PDF/Word export and trend charts are part of the next pass.
Reports.redFlags: {count, plural, one {# red flag} other {# red flags}}

Analytics.title: Analytics
Analytics.subtitle: Counts and coverage across your records.
Analytics.chartsWhat: Per-biomarker line charts with reference-range overlays, medication overlays, and symptom overlays (GOLD §5.9) are part of the next pass.
Analytics.chartsNext: Recharts-powered charts with hover, zoom, and date-range compare.

Symptoms.title: Symptoms
Symptoms.subtitle: Track how you feel over time (GOLD §5.10).
Symptoms.trackingTitle: Symptom tracking — {count, plural, one {# entry recorded} other {# entries recorded}}
Symptoms.trackingWhat: The schema and storage are ready (0–10 scoring across energy, mood, libido, sleep, and more). Data entry and trend charts are part of the next pass.
Symptoms.trackingNext: A daily check-in form and per-symptom trend charts overlaid with your labs.

Patients.title: Patient profile
Patients.subtitle: Your baseline data. Used only to give context to your labs.

Settings.title: Settings
Settings.subtitle: Account, appearance, and data controls.
Settings.appearanceDesc: Switch between light and dark mode.
Settings.accountDesc: Signed in as {email}. {count, plural, one {# audited action on record} other {# audited actions on record}}.
Settings.exportWhat: Right-to-be-forgotten and full data export (GOLD §8) are part of the next pass. Until then, contact your administrator to export or delete your data.
Settings.exportNext: One-click JSON export and verified account deletion that purges all patient data.

Report.title: Clinical Report
Report.dosingSub: {protocols, plural, one {# protocol} other {# protocols}} · {priority, plural, one {# clinical priority} other {# clinical priority}}
Report.insufficientData: Insufficient data to characterize this section.
Report.outOfRange: {biomarker} ({valueUnit}) is {dir, select, below {below} above {above} other {outside}} the reference range{refSuffix}.
Report.lhClausePresent: LH ({lhValueUnit}) helps contextualize whether this is primary or secondary in origin — a distinction for your clinician to make.
Report.lhClauseAbsent: No LH value is on record to contextualize this; consider discussing with your clinician.
Report.execSummaryBody: This report summarizes {classified, plural, one {# lab value} other {# lab values}}{hasDates, select, true { collected between {start} and {end}} other {}}. {hasRed, select, true {{redFlags, plural, one {# value warrants} other {# values warrant}} prompt clinician review. } other {}}{abnormal, plural, one {# value is} other {# values are}} outside, and {borderline, plural, one {# is} other {# are}} at the edge of, the reference range. This summary is educational and organizational; it does not diagnose or prescribe. Discuss all findings with your healthcare provider.
Report.followUpQuestion: How would you like to follow up?
Report.noRedFlagsFollowUp: No values crossed the prompt-review thresholds; what routine follow-up cadence do you recommend?
Report.lifestyleFactors: Sleep duration, exercise frequency, alcohol use, and body composition influence hormone and metabolic markers. Tracking these consistently over time improves the interpretation of trends.
Report.guidelineReference1: Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline on Testosterone Therapy in Adult Men with Androgen Deficiency Syndromes.
Report.guidelineReference2: AACE/ACE clinical guidance on hypogonadism and male sexual health (refer to current published editions for exact citations).

Findings.RF-HEMATOCRIT-HIGH: Hematocrit is recorded at {valueUnit}, at or above 54%. This warrants prompt clinician review (a common discussion point in TRT contexts).
Findings.RF-HEMOGLOBIN-HIGH: Hemoglobin is recorded at {valueUnit}, markedly elevated. Prompt clinician review is warranted.
Findings.RF-PSA-ELEVATED: PSA is recorded at {valueUnit}, at or above 4.0 ng/mL. Prompt clinician review is warranted.
Findings.RF-PSA-SIGNIFICANT: PSA is recorded at {valueUnit}, ≥ 10.0 ng/mL. Prompt clinician review is strongly advised.
Findings.RF-ALT-HIGH: ALT is recorded at {valueUnit}, markedly elevated. Prompt clinician review is warranted to assess liver function.
Findings.RF-AST-HIGH: AST is recorded at {valueUnit}, markedly elevated. Prompt clinician review is warranted to assess liver function.
Findings.RF-EGFR-LOW: eGFR is recorded at {valueUnit}, reduced. Prompt clinician review is warranted to assess kidney function.
Findings.RF-TRIGLYCERIDES-HIGH: Triglycerides are recorded at {valueUnit}, markedly elevated. Prompt clinician review is warranted.
Findings.RF-GLUCOSE-HIGH: Glucose is recorded at {valueUnit}, markedly elevated. Prompt clinician review is warranted.
Findings.PT-LOW-T: Recorded testosterone value(s) sit in the lower portion of or below the reference range: {hits}. {lhClause} This is an observation, not a diagnosis.
Findings.PT-HEMATOCRIT-RISE: Hematocrit is trending up (latest {valueUnit}). Rising hematocrit is a pattern worth monitoring with your clinician.
Findings.PT-SHBG-LOW: SHBG ({valueUnit}) is low; lower SHBG can raise the free hormone fraction. Worth discussing with your clinician when interpreting free testosterone.
Findings.PT-E2-HIGH: Estradiol ({valueUnit}) is above the reference range. A discussion point for your clinician, particularly in the context of any symptoms.
Findings.PT-ATHEROGENIC-LIPIDS: An atherogenic lipid pattern is present (multiple lipid values outside the reference range): {hits}. Worth discussing cardiovascular follow-up with your clinician.
Findings.PT-RENAL: Kidney-function markers are outside the reference range: {hits}. Worth discussing with your clinician.
Findings.PT-METABOLIC: Metabolic markers are elevated relative to reference: {hits}. Worth discussing metabolic health follow-up with your clinician.

Dosing.title: Dosing Recommendations
Dosing.desc: Exact steroid + ancillary dosages based on your lab results and clinical protocols. The physician validates every recommendation.
Dosing.summaryNote: These are recommendations based on retrieved clinical protocols (Graphiti RAG). The physician validates every dose, frequency, and cycle length before prescribing.

ManualEntry.save: Save {count, plural, one {# value} other {# values}}

Upload.dropHere: Drag & drop lab files here, or click to browse

LabsList.empty: No lab reports uploaded yet. Drop a file above to begin.

trenbolone_acetate.name: Trenbolone Acetate
trenbolone_acetate.expectedShift: +100–200 ng/dL testosterone, fat loss + muscle retention

primobolan_enanthate.name: Primobolan (Methenolone Enanthate)
primobolan_enanthate.expectedShift: lean mass preservation, low androgenic side effects

oxandrolone.name: Oxandrolone
oxandrolone.expectedShift: +5–10% lean mass, fat loss, strength gains

dianabol.name: Dianabol (Methandienone)
dianabol.expectedShift: rapid strength + mass gains, water retention

letrozole.name: Letrozole
letrozole.expectedShift: ↓ estradiol by 30–60 pg/mL (more potent than AI)

clenbuterol.name: Clenbuterol
clenbuterol.frequency: daily (2 weeks on, 2 weeks off — up-titrate)

trt_base.indication: Low total testosterone ({value} ng/dL) — below typical reference range
trt_base.notes: {noteKey, select, below_ref {Total testosterone below laboratory reference low — TRT indicated. Start at lower end of range, reassess at 12 weeks.} other {Total testosterone in borderline/low range — consider TRT if symptoms correlate.}}

e2_high.indication: Elevated estradiol ({value} pg/mL) — aromatization of testosterone
e2_high.notes: {noteKey, select, high {Estradiol significantly elevated — consider Letrozole (more potent AI).} upper {Estradiol at upper range — Anastrozole at low dose sufficient.} other {Estradiol mildly elevated — monitor, consider low-dose Anastrozole.}}

e2_low.indication: Estradiol low — consider reducing AI dose
e2_low.notes: If not already on an AI, no need to start. If on AI, consider dose reduction.

hct_high.notes: {noteKey, select, high {Hematocrit above upper reference — consider phlebotomy if >55%. Use lower TRT dose initially.} other {Hematocrit at upper range — monitor closely with TRT. Lower starting dose recommended.}}

shbg_high.indication: High SHBG ({value} nmol/L) — reduces free testosterone availability
shbg_high.notes: High SHBG binds testosterone, reducing free bioavailable T. Higher TRT dose may be needed.

nandrolone.indication: Normal total T but low free T or downward trend — Nandrolone may improve body composition with less aromatization
nandrolone.notes: Nandrolone is 19-nor — less aromatization than T. Monitor prolactin. May need hCG support.

boldenone.indication: Good testosterone + lipid imbalance — Boldenone for lean mass with moderate lipid impact
boldenone.notes: Boldenone has moderate lipid impact. Monitor LDL/HDL ratio. Less suppressive than Tren.

hcg_support.indication: Testicular volume preservation + intratesticular testosterone during TRT
hcg_support.notes: hCG mimics LH — maintains Leydig cell function. Essential if fertility is a goal.

clomiphene.indication: Post-TRT recovery or natural T stimulation alternative
clomiphene.notes: SERM — blocks estrogen at pituitary, ↑ LH/FSH, ↑ endogenous T. Use 4–12 weeks post-TRT.

clenbuterol.indication: Fat loss phase — beta-2 agonist increases metabolic rate
clenbuterol.notes: 2 weeks on / 2 weeks off to prevent receptor downregulation. Start low (20 mcg), titrate.

panel_completion.name: Panel Completion
panel_completion.notes: Full hormone panel (LH, FSH, prolactin), metabolic panel (A1C, glucose), lipid panel needed for optimal dosing.
panel_completion.expectedShift: Complete biomarker picture for accurate dosing
